scope_note: Input files are .gitignore configuration files, not prompt/command/agent .md files. Verbosity detection is adapted: comment blocks are treated as the equivalent of verbose sections. Line-count thresholds (critical=200, major=100, minor=50) are not applicable at these file sizes; percentage-based thresholds (critical>=30%, major>=15%, minor<15%) are used instead. All findings are tagged with introduced_in_diff per AC-12.1 Mechanism (ii).

verbose_sections.content_summary: 5-line comment block explaining why .taxonomy.json must be tracked, including references to UNESCO ISCED and Dewey Decimal systems. The rationale spans 3 explanatory sentences where a single-line note suffices.
ASCII art banner (============) followed by 2 lines of explanatory rationale for the PUBLIC REPOSITORY EXCLUSIONS section. Decorative separators are visual noise in .gitignore files.

findings.recommendation: Apply 'rules not stories' principle: condense the taxonomy warning to a single line (e.g., '# IMPORTANT: do not ignore knowledge-base/.taxonomy.json'). Remove the decorative ASCII separator; replace with a plain section header comment. Target reduction: 8 lines (9.3% -> ~1%). Reference: convert.md cleanup (commit 2d21631) reduced verbose explanatory sections by removing similar rationale prose.

verbose_sections.content_summary: Identical 5-line comment block to knowledge-system/.gitignore: explains why .taxonomy.json must be tracked with UNESCO ISCED and Dewey Decimal references. Copy-paste of verbose rationale prose.
Identical ASCII art banner and PUBLIC REPOSITORY EXCLUSIONS rationale as knowledge-system/.gitignore. Decorative separators and explanatory rationale duplicated across both files.

findings.recommendation: Apply 'rules not stories' principle: same fix as knowledge-system/.gitignore — condense taxonomy warning to 1 line, remove decorative ASCII separator. Additionally, the duplication across both knowledge-system files suggests a shared template source; fix the template. Target reduction: 8 lines (9.1% -> ~1%).

verbose_sections.content_summary: Comment block consists of an explanatory comment ('# Test files (keep demo file)') followed by two commented-out ignore patterns ('# *.xlsx', '# *.xls'). Commented-out rules are inert dead code; the explanation above them adds verbosity without function.

findings.recommendation: Remove the commented-out *.xlsx and *.xls patterns entirely rather than leaving them as dead comment-code. If the intent is to document an exception, use a negation pattern (e.g., '!demo.xlsx') which is functional. The explanatory comment alone (line 6) is sufficient if retained. Target reduction: 2 lines.

clean_files.verdict: clean — single-line section headers only, no verbose explanatory blocks
clean — concise section labels, no verbose blocks
clean — minimal, all comments are single-line labels
clean — concise section labels, one inline spec reference (acceptable)
clean — single-line section headers; first line references docs.github.com (acceptable URL reference)

summary.dominant_pattern: Multi-line explanatory comment blocks (taxonomy rationale, decorative ASCII separators) in knowledge-system files; commented-out dead-code patterns in excel-analyzer
summary.note: No findings reach major/critical thresholds. All violations are minor advisory. None of the files are prompt/command/agent .md files — verbosity violations apply only to inline comment blocks.
